description_long: This induction cooker features a rectangular, low-profile body with a muted greenish-gray finish. The top surface is equipped with two octagonal induction heating zones, each outlined with dark and light geometric segments, suggesting the placement for cookware. On the front face, there are two prominent black rotary knobs, each corresponding to one of the burners, allowing for manual adjustment of heat levels. The sides and rear of the cooker are plain, with no visible vents, handles, or additional controls. The overall design is minimalistic and utilitarian, suitable for portable or countertop use in urban environments such as street food stalls, garages, or temporary outdoor kitchens. No digital displays, brand markings, or power cords are visible, emphasizing a basic, functional aesthetic.
description: A compact, rectangular induction cooker with two octagonal burners and two front-facing rotary knobs, finished in muted greenish-gray tones. The design is minimalistic, with no visible digital display, branding, or power cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing two octagonal burners on top and two black rotary knobs on the front panel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the cooker and the side profile of the burners and knobs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain back with both burners visible, no controls or features present.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the side profile of the burners and the knobs protruding from the front.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel consists of two large, black rotary knobs symmetrically placed on the front face, each aligned below a burner. No labels, icons, indicator lights, or touch areas are visible.
